description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a circular stone base and a wooden gabled roof supported by four wooden posts. The stone well wall is thick and textured, showing signs of weathering and age, and sits atop a round, earthy platform that mimics natural ground. The wooden roof is constructed from wide, horizontal planks and is supported by a simple wooden frame, with a visible hand crank mechanism on one side for drawing water. The crank is attached to a horizontal wooden rod, suggesting a rope or chain would be used to lower and raise a bucket, though the bucket itself is not visible. The well is open at the top, allowing access to the interior, and the structure is robust, likely intended for outdoor use in parks or communal urban spaces. There are no visible decorative elements, and the design is utilitarian, focusing on function and durability.
description: A circular stone water well with a wooden gabled roof and a hand crank mechanism, set on a round earthy base. The design is rustic and functional, with weathered stone and wood.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open stone well, wooden roof, and hand crank mechanism on the right side.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the wooden support frame and the back of the stone well, with no crank visible.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the wooden roof, stone well, and the crank mechanism protruding from the left.
description_view_3: Opposite rear view focusing on the wooden frame and the back of the well, with the roof's peak visible.
attribute_well_cover_design: Wooden gabled roof with horizontal planks, no lid or grating over the well opening.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: Hand crank mechanism with a horizontal wooden rod, no visible bucket or pulley.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Circular stone wall approximately waist-high, with a wooden roof supported by posts.
